steps.title: Set up the theorem
steps.content:
For a right triangle, the legs and hypotenuse satisfy the Pythagorean theorem. Let $b$ be the unknown leg.
$$46.8^2+b^2=213.2^2$$

steps.title: Find the leg length
steps.content:
The length of the other leg is the positive square root.
$$b=\sqrt{43{,}264}=208$$
The answer is C.
